sentence_reviews.text: The learner predicts abstract target representations from context rather than reconstructing every low-level detail.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: JEPA arXiv abstract introduces I-JEPA as non-generative self-supervised learning that predicts representations of target blocks from a context block (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source directly supports prediction of target representations from context and non-generative/non-reconstruction framing.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Predictive representation learning / JEPA is to connect Self-supervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Joint-embedding predictive architectures.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: JEPA arXiv abstract describes I-JEPA as self-supervised learning of semantic image representations and as a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (lines 35-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports self-supervised representation learning and JEPA; the exact atlas graph-role wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: JEPA arXiv title and abstract introduce an Image-based Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (lines 35-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The example directly matches the inspected source.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: JEPA arXiv abstract calls I-JEPA a non-generative approach for self-supervised learning from images (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source directly supports the combination with self-supervised learning.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Self-supervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: JEPA arXiv abstract discusses masking strategy and context/target block choices as core design choices (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports that design choices matter, but not the full deployment-objective limitation or dependency list exactly.

sentence_reviews.text: Predictive representation learning / JEPA follows the learning setup expressed by Self-supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: JEPA arXiv abstract identifies I-JEPA as self-supervised learning from images (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source directly supports the trained-under relation.

sentence_reviews.text: Predictive representation learning / JEPA uses Embeddings & distributed representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: JEPA arXiv abstract says the method predicts representations of target blocks and learns semantic image representations (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The source directly supports representation/embedding use.

sentence_reviews.text: Predictive representation learning / JEPA uses Latent variables & hidden state as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: JEPA arXiv abstract discusses semantic image representations and target-block representations (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: Representations are supported, but the inspected evidence does not explicitly characterize them as latent variables or hidden state.

sentence_reviews.text: Predictive representation learning / JEPA uses Sequences, events & time series as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected JEPA evidence is image/block based and does not support sequences, events, or time series as a dependency.

edge_reviews.notes: I-JEPA is explicitly described as self-supervised learning; core strength is supported.
Prediction of target representations directly supports a core embeddings/representations dependency.
Representations are supported, but latent-variable wording is broader than the inspected evidence; common strength is plausible but not directly established.
The inspected source concerns images and spatial blocks, not sequences or time series.
